title: [PropertyDefinition]
text: [PropertyDefinition] This section, of which multiple are defined in the uproperties.ini file, defines a single property definition used by the Property Inspector of the DSP Component Editor in the IDE. Syntax Explain this code [PropertyId]= [html:title] description= Description category= Category tooltip= Tooltip datatype= DataType choice= Choice entries= Entries altchoice= AlternativeChoice Copy See the following table for syntax description: Syntax Description PropertyId Specifies the physical property id as it is maintained in the property list and supplied to your widget. The PropertyId is often prefixed with either html , style , or class separated by a colon. Other prefixes are also allowed but do not have a special treatment in Uniface. For example: html:title . The grp keyword is not allowed, as this is reserved for property groups. The following keywords are not allowed as PropertyId : propertydatatypes , propertygroups . The PropertyId is always send in lower case to the browser, regardless of how it is defined. It is good practice to not use camelcase for PropertyIds , but instead use hyphens (-) or underscores (_) as word separators. Description Specifies a more descriptive name or description of the property. It should be short, a single line, and is allowed to contain spaces. Category Specifies in which tab (category) the property will appear in the Property Inspector. Tooltip Specifies a more extensive description of the property. It might include a syntax or a reference to a standard W3C website describing the property. Sometimes it is useful to provide some examples. Use a semicolon to break your tooltip into multiple lines. Datatype Specifies the editing experience provided by the Property Inspector. Possible data types are defined in the [propertydatatypes] section of the uproperties.ini file. Choice Specifies a semicolon-separated list of acceptable values — only for use with data types that use the dropdown-box widget type. Entries Specifies the number of entries the Property Editor will accept. Typically used for shorthand style definitions where multiple values of the same type specify for example the top, bottom, left, and right border. If not specifies, only 1 entry is allowed. AlternativeChoice Specifies a semicolon-separated list of keywords that the Property Editor will also accept as value, for example "inherited", "unset", "default". For more details, see: web.ini uproperties.ini Component Editor Example 1: Explain this code [html:title] description=Title (Tooltip Text) category=Html tooltip=The title attribute specifies extra information about an element. The information is most often shown as a tooltip text when the mouse hovers over the element. datatype=string Copy Example 2: Explain this code [html:type] description=Type category=Html tooltip=The type attribute specifies function of the input element. datatype=choice choice=button;checkbox;color;date;datetime;email;file;hidden;month;number;password;radio;range;search;tel;text;time;url;week Copy Example 3: Explain this code [style:flex-shrink] description=Flex Shrink category=Flex Item tooltip=Sets the shrink factor of a flex item. datatype=positive_float altchoice=inherit;initial;revert;revert-layer;unset Copy Related Links uproperties.ini The uproperties.ini file defines the syntax of properties as well as property groups for web widgets.
